I have strong 2026 SOTA grounding. The decisive references: CiteAudit (multi-agent calibrated verdicts for citation faithfulness), the "Probabilistic Guarantees" paper (ensemble/majority-vote over independent judge calls drives error rate down exponentially), and the documented unreliability of single LLM-judge calls (position/verbosity/self-enhancement bias, confident-but-wrong). The SOTA pattern that bites POLARIS's gap: route-up + ensemble + abstain-and-flag on judge uncertainty/error, never fail-open.

I have everything needed. Returning the finding.

DIMENSION: citation_faithfulness_at_scale

CURRENT_STATE: POLARIS verifies faithfulness per delivered sentence in two layers. (1) strict_verify (src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:204-291) runs mechanical checks (a)-(e): provenance token present, evidence-id in pool, span bounds valid, every decimal in the sentence appears in the cited span, ≥2 shared content words. These are topical/numeric HYGIENE, not correctness. (2) Check (f), the only real faithfulness test, is the LLM entailment judge (strict_verify.py:279-289), default mode "enforce" (strict_verify.py:173) — for every cited sentence it calls google/gemma-4-31b-it via OpenRouter to decide ENTAILED/NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED and DROPS on NEUTRAL or CONTRADICTED. CRITICAL: the judge (src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:259-261) FAILS OPEN — any non-budget exception (HTTP error, timeout, JSON parse failure, bad verdict string at :252-253) returns ("ENTAILED", "judge_error: ..."). The gate at strict_verify.py:283 branches ONLY on verdict in ("NEUTRAL","CONTRADICTED"), so a fail-open "ENTAILED" falls straight through to `return True, None` (PASS). _record_judge_outcome ticks the judge_error telemetry counter (entailment_judge.py:362-364) but the SENTENCE STILL SHIPS unverified — strict_verify itself never surfaces the error to the manifest. A separate advisory NLI second-validator path (src/polaris_graph/retrieval/nli_benchmark_annotator.py:102-199) uses the SAME judge and DOES expose judge_error_count + nli_status:"error" (:156-184), but it is advisory-only and never changes release_allowed/status.

IS_WIRED_IN_BENCHMARK_PATH: yes — Gate-B forces PG_NLI_IN_BENCHMARK=1 (scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:676) and PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT defaults to "enforce" (strict_verify.py:173), so both the binding entailment gate and the advisory NLI annotator are reached by run_gate_b_query -> run_one_query -> per-section strict_verify + the post-gen NLI block (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:5111-5181). This is NOT a built-but-not-wired case; the gap is behavioral-at-scale.

LATEST_BEST_PRACTICE: 2026 SOTA for citation-faithfulness verification at scale rejects single-call fail-open judging. (1) CiteAudit (arxiv 2602.23452) decomposes citation verification into a multi-agent pipeline (Claim Extractor -> Retriever -> Evidence Matcher -> Reasoner -> Judge) that emits CALIBRATED verdicts with explanations, treating an unresolved/uncertain verdict as a flag, not a pass. (2) "Probabilistic Guarantees for Reducing Contextual Hallucinations" (arxiv 2601.00641) shows a single imperfect judge's false-negative probability is bounded only by MAJORITY VOTE over independent judge calls, with ensemble error decreasing exponentially in the number of votes. (3) 2026 LLM-as-judge guidance (Label Your Data; Datadog) documents that single judge calls are systematically unreliable (position bias ~40% inconsistency, verbosity/self-enhancement bias, confident-but-wrong), and production systems ROUTE-UP ambiguous cases to a frontier judge and explicitly ABSTAIN-AND-FLAG when uncertain. MiniCheck (arxiv 2404.10774) is the cheap deterministic claim-level fact-checker used as a first-pass before the expensive judge. The unifying principle POLARIS violates: a verifier error must FAIL CLOSED (drop or flag the claim), never fail open to "verified."

GAP: At 1000-URL scale the delivered-sentence count grows large (PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_TO_EXTRACT=1500, multi-section reports), so the enforce-mode judge makes hundreds to low-thousands of sequential frontier-LLM calls per query. Transient OpenRouter errors (timeout at 30s, 429/5xx, empty 200, malformed JSON) are near-certain at that call volume — and EACH error makes one clinical claim PASS the binding faithfulness gate UNVERIFIED via the fail-open path, with no manifest signal that it happened (only a process-lifetime telemetry counter nobody reads at run end). The specific failure at full cap: a run can be reported `success` with release_allowed=true while N% of its cited sentences were never actually entailment-checked — the binding gate silently degraded exactly when volume made errors common. Secondary (one line): the advisory NLI step is the LAST post-gen step and re-raises BudgetExceededError (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:5161-5165), so at scale an advisory annotation can abort an otherwise-successful run on a late cap breach.

SEVERITY: P1 (edges toward P0 in the §-1.1 clinical frame). It is error-conditional, not unconditional, which keeps it from a clean P0 — but at 1000-sentence volume transient judge errors are statistically near-certain, the degradation is SILENT in the manifest, and each silent pass is an unverified clinical claim (wrong dose/contraindication surviving the only correctness gate). The "lethal in clinical context" framing applies directly.

PROPOSED_FIX: Make the binding entailment gate FAIL CLOSED on judge error. (1) In strict_verify.py:281-289, detect `reason.startswith("judge_error:")` and, in enforce mode, return (False, "entailment_unverified") — drop the sentence rather than pass it (matches the §9.1 "drop on failure" contract; an unverified claim is treated like an unsupported one). Add a "warn-degrade" sub-mode if operators need fail-open explicitly, but default must be fail-closed. (2) Surface judge_error_count into manifest from the BINDING path (not just the advisory NLI path) and add a run-level gate: if judge_error_rate exceeds a configurable floor (e.g. PG_MAX_JUDGE_ERROR_RATE), abort with abort_verifier_degraded rather than report success. (3) SOTA upgrade: add bounded retry (e.g. 2 retries with backoff) before declaring judge_error, and for high-stakes claims route-up / majority-vote over 3 independent judge calls per arxiv 2601.00641. All env-gated (LAW VI).

SMOKE_TEST (offline, pure, no network): monkeypatch `strict_verify._get_judge().judge` (re-exported, propagates) to return `("ENTAILED", "judge_error: simulated_timeout")`, build an EvidencePool whose cited span actually NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTS the sentence (e.g. span "GLP-1 RAs", sentence "semaglutide reduced HbA1c 1.8%"), set PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce, call `verify_sentence(sentence, pool)` and assert it returns `(True, None)` TODAY — demonstrating the silent fail-open pass-through. After the fix, the same test asserts `(False, "entailment_unverified")`. ~30 lines, runs in the existing tests/polaris_graph/ harness with no torch/no API key.

EVIDENCE_AUDIT (per CLAUDE.md §-1.1, claim-by-claim vs cited source): For the END-RESULT report.md, do NOT trust the manifest's verified counts. Read manifest.json's nli_verification.judge_error_count AND re-derive the binding-gate error rate from logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl (filter call_type=="entailment_judge" for the run's session_id, count entries whose response was degraded) — any nonzero judge_error count means some delivered sentences passed the binding gate unverified, so those exact sentences must be re-audited by hand. Then, claim-by-claim: take each cited sentence from report.md, resolve its [#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>] token to the exact span_quote in the evidence pool, and independently re-run the entailment decision (Claude + Codex in parallel per §-1.1.5), assigning VERIFIED/PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED with the cited span text quoted verbatim. Apply GRADE/AMSTAR-2 per clinical claim. The string-presence "X% verified" number in the manifest is exactly the banned metadata signal — the audit must be the per-claim entailment re-check against the fetched span, because that is precisely the check that silently failed open at scale.

Grounded file:line cites: strict_verify.py:173 (enforce default), :281-289 (gate branch only on NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED), entailment_judge.py:259-261 (fail-open to ENTAILED), :252-253 (bad-verdict also fails open), :362-364 (judge_error telemetry ticked but sentence passes), run_gate_b.py:676 (PG_NLI_IN_BENCHMARK force-on), run_honest_sweep_r3.py:5111-5181 (advisory NLI block, the only place judge_error is manifest-surfaced).

Sources: [CiteAudit](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.23452), [Probabilistic Guarantees for Reducing Contextual Hallucinations](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.00641), [LLM as a Judge: A 2026 Guide (Label Your Data)](https://labelyourdata.com/articles/llm-as-a-judge), [MiniCheck](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2404.10774), [Detecting hallucinations with LLM-as-a-judge (Datadog)](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/ai/llm-hallucination-detection/)
